title: Images — Maximum parallel image pulls
text:
When
serializeImagePulls
is set to false, the kubelet defaults to no limit on
the maximum number of images being pulled at the same time. If you would like to
limit the number of parallel image pulls, you can set the field
maxParallelImagePulls
in the kubelet configuration. With
maxParallelImagePulls
set to
n
, only
n
images can be pulled at the same time, and any image pull beyond
n
will have to
wait until at least one ongoing image pull is complete.
Limiting the number of parallel image pulls prevents image pulling from consuming
too much network bandwidth or disk I/O, when parallel image pulling is enabled.
You can set
maxParallelImagePulls
to a positive number that is greater than or
equal to 1. If you set
maxParallelImagePulls
to be greater than or equal to 2,
you must set
serializeImagePulls
to false. The kubelet will fail to start
with an invalid
maxParallelImagePulls
setting.
